title: viola canina subsp. silvatica
intro: Viola canina subsp. silvatica, also known as the wild pansy or field pansy, is a sub-species of the viola canina plant, native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of glycosides and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. silvatica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Viola canina subsp. silvatica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions.
